initial_program:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Row and column indices
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Output cell must exist for every cell
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Determine orientation:
% orient_rows is true if the grid has more rows than columns
orient_rows :- R > C.
orient_cols :- C > R.

% Exactly one of the two orientations holds
1 { orient_rows : R > C } 1 { orient_cols : C > R }.

% Determine the integer k = floor(larger / smaller)
% larger = max(R,C) and smaller = min(R,C)
% For rows orientation: larger = R, smaller = C
k(K) :- orient_rows, K*C <= R, (K+1)*C > R.
% For columns orientation: larger = C, smaller = R
k(K) :- orient_cols, K*R <= C, (K+1)*R > C.

% Period = 2 * k  (period is always even)
period(P) :- k(K), P = 2*K.

% List each nonzero cell
colored(R0,C0,Color) :- input(R0,C0,Color).

%--- Orientation: rows -------------------------------------------------
% Map a residue S to a colour for row orientation
% There is a colour for every residue that is the residue of a coloured cell
stripe_color_rows(S, Color) :- colored(R0,C0,Color), orient_rows,
                         period(P), S, Q, R0 = P*Q + S.

% Fill all rows that have this residue with that colour
output(R,C,Color) :- stripe_color_rows(S,Color), row(R), col(C),
                     1 { Q : R = P*Q + S }.

%--- Orientation: columns ----------------------------------------------
% Map a residue S to a colour for column orientation
stripe_color_cols(S, Color) :- colored(R0,C0,Color), orient_cols,
                         period(P), S, Q, C0 = P*Q + S.

% Fill all columns that have this residue with that colour
output(R,C,Color) :- stripe_color_cols(S,Color), row(R), col(C),
                     1 { Q : C = P*Q + S }.

% Exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Row and column indices
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Output cell must exist for every cell
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Determine orientation:
% orient_rows is true if the grid has more rows than columns
orient_rows :- R > C.
orient_cols :- C > R.

% Exactly one of the two orientations holds
1 { orient_rows : R > C }.
1 { orient_cols : C > R }.
% Determine the integer k = floor(larger / smaller)
% larger = max(R,C) and smaller = min(R,C)
% For rows orientation: larger = R, smaller = C
k(K) :- orient_rows, K*C <= R, (K+1)*C > R.
% For columns orientation: larger = C, smaller = R
k(K) :- orient_cols, K*R <= C, (K+1)*R > C.

% Period = 2 * k  (period is always even)
period(P) :- k(K), P = 2*K.

% List each nonzero cell
colored(R0,C0,Color) :- input(R0,C0,Color).

%--- Orientation: rows -------------------------------------------------
% Map a residue S to a colour for row orientation
% There is a colour for every residue that is the residue of a coloured cell
stripe_color_rows(S, Color) :- colored(R0,C0,Color), orient_rows,
stripe_color_rows(S, Color) :- colored(R0,C0,Color), orient_rows, period(P), R0 = P*Q + S.

% Fill all rows that have this residue with that colour
output(R,C,Color) :- stripe_color_rows(S,Color), row(R), col(C).
1 { Q : R = P*Q + S }.

%--- Orientation: columns ----------------------------------------------
% Map a residue S to a colour for column orientation
stripe_color_cols(S, Color) :- colored(R0,C0,Color), orient_cols,
                         period(P), S, Q, C0 = P*Q + S.

% Fill all columns that have this residue with that colour
output(R,C,Color) :- stripe_color_cols(S,Color), row(R), col(C),
output(R,C,Color) :- stripe_color_cols(S,Color), row(R), col(C).
1 { Q : C = P*Q + S }.
% Exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) }.
1 :- output_cell(R,C).
#show output/3.

col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

orient_rows :- R > C.
orient_cols :- C > R.

% Determine the integer k = floor(larger / smaller)

k(K) :- orient_rows, K*C <= R, (K+1)*C > R.
% For columns orientation: larger = C, smaller = R

% Period = 2 * k  (period is always even)
period(P) :- k(K), P = 2*K.
